rulings.Case Title: LINDA CATRON VS. COUNTRYWIDE BANK, FSB ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Leave To Amend And File First Amended Complaint **Filed Pursuant To Litigants Request**
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 31, 2024 line 1. PLAINTIFF LINDA CATRON Motion For Leave To Amend And File First Amended Complaint **Filed Pursuant To Litigants Request** is OFF CALENDAR. No proof of service on file. Notice of Appeal on file. The Court also notes that while the motion is titled "motion for leave to amend," the body of the motion seeks a continuance "of this hearing," without any explanation as to what hearing Plaintiff seeks to continue. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: LEANA PEREZ ET AL VS. BIANA CHERNOGUZ, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS TRUSTEE OF ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT To Cross-Complainants Second Amended Cross-Complaint Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 31, 2024 line 2. CROSS DEFENDANT LEANA PEREZ, FRANCISCO ARMAS, SERGIO ARMAS, ANTHONY ARMAS, FRANCES ARMAS MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT To Cross-Complainants Second Amended Cross-Complaint Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication is Denied. Motion for Summary Adjudication is DENIED. Moving party failed to comply with CRC 3.1350(b). Motion for Summary Judgment is DENIED. Moving party failed to shift their burden and establish that they are entitled to a judgment as a matter of law. Moving party's proposition that they should prevail on the cause of action for holdover damages because in order for the landlord to prevail on an independent lawsuit for holdover damages, landlord must first prevail on a UD case is not supported by any authority. The three cases relied upon in the moving papers do not support this statement. In fact, in the Court expressly held that a landlord, who does not obtain a judgment for possession in an unlawful detainer action "has other remedies available by which he can collect the unpaid rents." Moving party failed to show that this action is not one of such remedies. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: LEANA PEREZ ET AL VS. BIANA CHERNOGUZ, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS TRUSTEE OF ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 31, 2024 line 2. CROSS DEFENDANT LEANA PEREZ, FRANCISCO ARMAS, SERGIO ARMAS, ANTHONY ARMAS, FRANCES ARMAS DEMURRER to Amended CROSS COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. Demurrer is overruled as to the first cause of action, which is sufficiently stated. Demurrer is SUSTAINED without leave to amend as to the second cause of action for breach of oral contract. Cross-Complaint suggests that the oral contact was breached in 2015 (SAXC 10:26 "holdover subtenants in 2015 and by breached their lawful obligations as tenants). This cross-action was initiated on March 27, 2023 seeking tort damages based on "unlawful detainment" from February 1, 2020, i.e. 5 years after the purported breaches of the oral contract and no contract damages. (See Prayer, SAXC 11:9-18). The Court has previously granted a leave to amend "to address in good faith issues raised in the demurrer," Cross-Complainants chose not to avail themselves of this opportunity having added 2 conclusory statements (See last sentence of paragraph 22 and paragraph 29), with paragraph 29 being outside of the scope of the leave to amend. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 31, 2024 line 3. DEFENDANT BRIAN GROSS , AN INDIVIDUAL, BRIAN GROSS FINE ART, INC. rulings.Case Title: MORPHOS GALLERY, INC., DBA CATHARINE CLARK ET AL VS. DAVID A. KLEIN ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 31, 2024 line 3. DEFENDANT DAVID KLEIN , AN INDIVIDUAL DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with leave to amend (except for cause of action for Intentional Infliction of Emotional Distress) for Plaintiff to allege facts, not conclusions to support each element of each cause of action as to the moving defendant. Plaintiff must also allege facts supporting tolling/delayed discovery in light of the current allegations in the operative complaint that (1) on the one hand some identified person in an identified manner at an unidentified time represented that rent under the sublease is proportional to the sub-lease space; and (2) on the other hand a copy of the Master Lease presented to the Plaintiff had the rent amount redacted (which supposedly should have been double of the sublease rent). All new allegations must be in bold font. Demurrer to the cause of action for intentional infliction of emotional distress is sustained without leave to amend. No opposition filed. The Court notes that the Plaintiff has previously disregarding the Court's requirement for the amendments to be "fact" and to be "in bold." Plaintiff is ordered to comply with the requirement this time. The Cour further notes that Plaintiff's opposition is 13 pages long containing no Table of Contents or Table of Authorities. Failure to comply with CRC in future will result in briefs being stricken. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: ANDREW KING VS. THEODORE J. GUARRIELLO JR. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Expunge Lis Pendens; Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff And His Counsel; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declarations Of Chris Boulter And Jonathan D. Fink
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 31, 2024 line 5. DEFENDANT THEODORE GUARRIELLO , TRUSTEE OF THE THEODORE J. GUARRIELLO, JR. REVOCABLE TRUST DATED 6/18/2003, AND RESTATED 6/29/2006, BRET GREENBERG, TIMOTHY J. SPAETH AND ANDREA N. SPAETH, TRUSTEES, PACIFIC PREMIER TRUST, CUSTODIAN FBO BRET, VAL-CHRIS INVESTMENTS, INC., PACIFIC PREMIER TRUST, CUSTODIAN FBO BRUCE BUCKLEY, TJ GUARRIELLO, JR. FAMILY FOUNDATION, BRUCE Notice Of Motion And Motion To Expunge Lis Pendens; Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff And His Counsel; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declarations Of Chris Boulter And Jonathan D. Fink is GRANTED. Plaintiff failed to show a probable validity of a real property claim in the original complaint upon which the lis pendens was recorded or in the First Amended Complaint filed during the pendency of the motion to expunge. The Court notes that Plaintiff failed to comply with CCP 405.22 creating unnecessary confusion and delay. While this failure does not form the basis of the decision on the merits, Plaintiff in their counsel are ordered to comply with ALL procedural and substantive requirements of the state and local statutes and rules. Request for attorney's fees to be paid by the Plaintiff in the amount of $7,046.50 is granted. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
